runtime.title: Live / Paper Runtime Status
runtime.subtitlePost: . This page does not authorize connectors, start runners, stop runners, submit orders, cancel orders, or change mandates.
runtime.unavailableHint: Treat connector runtime as unavailable until the backend provides a current status snapshot.
runtime.noProfilesBody: The backend did not return any recognized live or paper broker profiles.
runtime.riskHaltedDesc: The kill switch is tripped for this profile. Treat connector runtime as stopped until status clears.
runtime.riskActiveDesc: Runner is alive and an unexpired mandate is present. This page is still read-only status only.
runtime.riskIdleDesc: Authorization and mandate are present, but the runner is not alive.
runtime.riskDormantDesc: Connector runtime is not ready for autonomous operation because authorization, mandate, or runner liveness is missing.

reports.title: Backtest Report Library
reports.subtitle: Browse historical backtest reports, metrics, and run details from one place.
reports.searchPlaceholder: Search run id, prompt, symbol, status...
reports.emptyBody: Create a backtest from Agent, then return here to review it.
reports.noMatchesBody: Adjust your search query, status filter, or date range.

welcome.title: Vibe-Trading
welcome.subtitle: vibe trading with your professional financial agent team
welcome.describePrompt: Describe a trading strategy to get started.

quickStart.title: Start with a common research flow
quickStart.subtitle: Open a Session around your real portfolio, one security, or a durable research task.

portfolioAnalysis.title: Portfolio health check
portfolioAnalysis.description: Read real holdings and cash, then assess returns, concentration, and risk.
portfolioAnalysis.eyebrow: Real holdings · Fresh quotes · Prioritized actions
portfolioAnalysis.detail1: Use the portfolio ledger as truth and refresh decision-critical quotes first
portfolioAnalysis.detail2: Break down P&L, position concentration, sector exposure, and style exposure
portfolioAnalysis.detail3: Combine catalysts, news, and risk into a prioritized action list
portfolioAnalysis.footer: Research only: it will not place orders or change holdings. Price-sensitive conclusions stop when the data is too weak.
portfolioAnalysis.prompt: Analyze my current real portfolio and cash. Use portfolio_state as the source of truth, refresh decision-critical market data first, then assess performance, concentration, sector and style exposure, catalysts, and risks. Give prioritized actions, but do not place orders or modify holdings.

stockAnalysis.title: Quick stock analysis
stockAnalysis.description: Add a company name or ticker to review trend, news, valuation, and risk.
stockAnalysis.eyebrow: Identity check · Latest data · Scenario view
stockAnalysis.detail1: Resolve the exact security first to avoid market or naming ambiguity
stockAnalysis.detail2: Cover recent trend, news catalysts, fundamentals, and valuation context
stockAnalysis.detail3: Show upside, base, and risk scenarios without widening to other holdings
stockAnalysis.footer: This inserts a guided prompt into the composer so you can add the company name or ticker before sending.
stockAnalysis.prompt: Analyze [company name or ticker]. First resolve the exact security and verify the latest available data, then cover price trend, news and catalysts, fundamentals, valuation, key risks, and scenarios. Do not widen this into my other holdings.

deepReport.title: Equity deep research
deepReport.description: Create a standardized single-stock report with evidence and deterministic calculations.
deepReport.eyebrow: Listed company · Evidence chain · PDF report
deepReport.detail1: Read through all three financial statements and flag financial-quality checks
deepReport.detail2: Use deterministic calculations to test implied valuation and operating scenarios
deepReport.detail3: Preserve an Evidence → Fact → Claim chain and produce a reusable report
deepReport.footer: Best for reviewable listed-company research. Modules with insufficient evidence are explicitly downgraded.

marketReview.title: Pre / post-market review
marketReview.description: Combine holdings, overnight markets, and news into the next-session watch list.
marketReview.detail1: Filter for changes directly relevant to current holdings and watch themes
marketReview.detail2: Summarize overnight US, macro, sector, and company-news impact
marketReview.detail3: Rank watch items, triggers, and risks to avoid in the next session
marketReview.footer: Uses Beijing time and the latest trading day by default, with timestamps and source limits called out.
marketReview.prompt: Create a pre-market or post-market review for my real portfolio. Identify the latest trading day in Beijing time, refresh key quotes first, then combine overnight US markets, macro, sector, and company news. Include only changes directly relevant to my holdings, and rank next-session watch items, triggers, and key risks. Do not place orders.

allocationPlan.title: Portfolio allocation plan
allocationPlan.description: Compare add, rebalance, and wait options under cash, horizon, and risk constraints.
allocationPlan.eyebrow: Cash constraint · Holding horizon · Plan comparison
allocationPlan.detail1: Read existing positions so suggestions reflect the real concentration profile
allocationPlan.detail2: Set constraints from available cash, holding horizon, and drawdown tolerance
allocationPlan.detail3: Compare staged deployment, one-time deployment, and keeping cash
allocationPlan.footer: Produces research scenarios, allocation ranges, and triggers; it does not execute trades.
allocationPlan.prompt: Build a portfolio allocation plan from my real holdings and cash. Read current positions first, then ask for or confirm deployable cash, holding horizon, and maximum acceptable drawdown. Compare staged deployment, one-time deployment, and keeping cash, with allocation ranges, triggers, and risks. Do not execute trades.

researchGoal.title: Create a research goal
researchGoal.description: Pin an open question as a multi-turn goal with criteria and an evidence ledger.
researchGoal.detail1: Turn an open question into concrete, reviewable completion criteria
researchGoal.detail2: Keep every later turn on the same goal state and prioritize missing evidence
researchGoal.detail3: Track contradictions, caveats, evidence counts, and the completion audit
researchGoal.footer: Best for research that cannot be completed in one answer and needs to converge over time.

exampleHints.subtitle: These presets run immediately; hover or focus first to review the method, output, and boundaries.

multiMarketBacktest.eyebrow: Cross-market data · Strategy rules · Benchmark comparison
multiMarketBacktest.detail1: Lock securities, date range, frequency, and rebalancing rules to keep the setup consistent
multiMarketBacktest.detail2: Show return, drawdown, volatility, Sharpe, and differences versus the benchmark
multiMarketBacktest.detail3: State data sources, transaction costs, slippage, and sample limitations
multiMarketBacktest.footer: Backtests study historical scenarios; they do not guarantee future returns or connect to live order placement.

researchAnalysis.eyebrow: Model assumptions · Metric breakdown · Sensitivity checks
researchAnalysis.detail1: Expose factors, pricing formulas, and input parameters instead of hiding key assumptions
researchAnalysis.detail2: Break down signal contribution, risk exposure, and sensitivity to parameter changes
researchAnalysis.detail3: Separate model output, verifiable facts, and judgments that still need evidence
researchAnalysis.footer: Best for model and derivatives scenarios; model output is not a guaranteed return.

swarmTeams.eyebrow: Specialist roles · Parallel research · Unified review
swarmTeams.detail1: Assign research, risk, quant, and portfolio-manager roles to match the task
swarmTeams.detail2: Run independent steps in parallel while preserving dependencies between stages
swarmTeams.detail3: Use a synthesis role to reconcile disagreement, evidence gaps, and the final conclusion
swarmTeams.footer: Best for complex work that needs cross-checks; it usually takes longer and uses more model calls than normal chat.

docWebResearch.eyebrow: Document parsing · Live sources · Evidence citations
docWebResearch.detail1: Extract evidence directly relevant to the question from uploaded files or live pages
docWebResearch.detail2: Record source, publication time, and data cutoff so old and new information are not mixed
docWebResearch.detail3: Separate facts, summaries, and inference while calling out evidence gaps
docWebResearch.footer: PDF examples require an upload first; web research prioritizes verifiable sources and states freshness limits.

tradeJournal.eyebrow: Trade records · Statistical profile · Behavior diagnosis
tradeJournal.detail1: Normalize executions, holdings, and P&L from supported broker exports
tradeJournal.detail2: Calculate win rate, payoff ratio, holding period, time-of-day, and symbol distribution
tradeJournal.detail3: Use numeric evidence to diagnose disposition, overtrading, chasing, and anchoring
tradeJournal.footer: Upload a journal first; the diagnosis supports behavioral review and is not a complete investment decision.

tradingConnectors.eyebrow: Profile checks · Account reads · Read-only market data
tradingConnectors.detail1: Confirm the selected connector and its authentication, account, and data status
tradingConnectors.detail2: Read account summaries, positions, cash, quotes, and bars only when requested
tradingConnectors.detail3: Return concrete missing setup when a connection fails instead of silently returning empty data
tradingConnectors.footer: These examples stay read-only and never create, modify, or cancel orders.

shadowAccount.eyebrow: Rule extraction · Shadow backtest · Delta attribution
shadowAccount.detail1: Extract reviewable entry, exit, and sizing rules from the trading journal
shadowAccount.detail2: Compare the shadow strategy and actual trading over the same period
shadowAccount.detail3: Attribute the gap to rule violations, early exits, missed signals, and other causes
shadowAccount.footer: A shadow account is a research and review model, not a live account, and it never executes trades.

examples.crossMarketPortfolioDesc: A-shares + crypto + US equities with risk-parity optimizer
examples.btcMacdDesc: Minute-level crypto backtest with real-time OKX data
examples.usTechMaxDivDesc: Portfolio optimizer across FAANG+ via yfinance
examples.investmentCommitteeDesc: Multi-agent debate: long vs short, risk review, PM decision
examples.quantStrategyDeskDesc: Screening → factor research → backtest → risk audit pipeline
examples.earningsReportDesc: Upload a PDF and ask questions about the financials
examples.macroResearchDesc: Read live web sources for macro analysis
examples.analyzeBrokerExportDesc: Parse 同花顺/东财/富途/generic CSV — holding days, win rate, PnL ratio, hourly distribution
examples.diagnoseBehaviorDesc: Disposition effect, overtrading, chasing momentum, anchoring — severity + numeric evidence
examples.checkConnectorDesc: List connector profiles and verify the selected one
examples.analyzePortfolioDesc: Read account summary and positions from the selected connector
examples.quoteTrendDesc: Fetch a quote plus recent daily bars through the selected connector
examples.trainShadowDesc: Extract your strategy rules from a broker CSV and persist a Shadow profile
examples.shadowDelta: How Much Am I Leaving on the Table?
examples.shadowDeltaDesc: Backtest your shadow strategy and attribute delta vs. your actual PnL
examples.shadowReportDesc: 8-section HTML/PDF — equity curve, per-market Sharpe, attribution waterfall

settings.title: Settings
settings.authDesc: Enter the API auth key to unlock settings.
settings.dataSourcesDesc: Configure external data sources for the backtesting engine.
settings.tushareDesc: China A-share historical data (requires TUSHARE_TOKEN env var).
settings.baostockDesc: Alternative A-share data via TCP protocol (no API key needed).

codexCli.title: Deep research executor
codexCli.description: One Deep Report framework can run through the current Provider or an isolated Codex CLI process.
codexCli.providerDescription: Recommended for normal use. Reuses the selected model, streaming, usage tracking, and the in-app AgentLoop.
codexCli.cliDescription: Advanced mode for long, high-assurance jobs with an ephemeral sandbox. Its model and reasoning effort are configured separately below.
codexCli.providerSelected: Deep research now uses the current Provider
codexCli.cliSelected: Deep research now uses the isolated Codex CLI
codexCli.modelSettingsDescription: These preferences apply only to isolated deep research. They use the same OpenAI Codex model catalog and refresh flow as Provider settings, but are saved independently and do not inherit the active Provider model.
codexCli.modelHint: Select a model available to the isolated Codex account.
codexCli.reasoningEffortHint: Higher effort takes longer and uses more quota.
codexCli.modelSettingsSaveFailed: Unable to save isolated CLI model settings
codexCli.modelRefreshFailed: Unable to refresh isolated CLI models
codexCli.oneTimeHint: Each research request starts a fresh, isolated, one-off Codex CLI task. The task exits when it finishes, while its report, evidence, and revision history remain saved in Vibe-Trading.
codexCli.copiedPowerShell: Command copied. Paste it into Windows PowerShell to run it.
codexCli.copiedTerminal: Command copied. Paste it into the server terminal to run it.
codexCli.saveFailed: Unable to save deep research executor
codexCli.remoteHint: Run the copied device-login command on the host running Vibe-Trading. A remote browser cannot open that host's terminal.
codexCli.commandLocationTitle: Where should I paste the copied command?
codexCli.commandLocationPowerShell: Paste it into Windows PowerShell and press Enter. Do not paste it into the browser address bar, chat box, or traditional Command Prompt (CMD).
codexCli.commandLocationTerminal: Paste it into the terminal on the host running Vibe-Trading and press Enter. Do not paste it into the browser or chat box.
codexCli.isolationHint: The isolated task does not read global credentials, config, AGENTS, other Codex sessions, plugins, apps, or user skills; shell and built-in web search are also disabled.

channels.title: IM Channels
channels.description: Connect chat adapters to the same queued, research-only session runtime used by the Web UI.

researchGoalHint.title: What is a research goal?
researchGoalHint.eyebrow: Session goal · Multi-turn progress · Evidence ledger
researchGoalHint.description: Pins an open-ended question to the current session as a persistent goal. The app creates completion criteria and starts the first research turn immediately; later turns keep reading the same goal state instead of treating it as a one-off question.
researchGoalHint.capability1: Tracks the objective, completion criteria, and evidence count in an expandable progress panel
researchGoalHint.capability2: Prioritizes uncovered criteria while recording contradictions, caveats, and risk boundaries
researchGoalHint.capability3: Supports continue, edit, and cancel; completion requires an audit against the evidence ledger
researchGoalHint.footer: Open-ended work that needs several evidence-gathering turns and a durable record of progress.

agent.describeGoal: Describe the research goal to attach to this session
agent.placeholder: e.g. Create a dual MA crossover strategy for 000001.SZ, backtest 2024

swarmTeamHint.title: What is a swarm team?
swarmTeamHint.eyebrow: 30 team presets · DAG workflow · Unified synthesis
swarmTeamHint.description: Selects the closest preset from the task intent, then assigns work to specialist roles by dependency. Independent tasks can run in parallel before a synthesis role produces the final, consistent result.
swarmTeamHint.capability1: Includes 30 teams for equities, macro, quant, risk, funds, derivatives, and more
swarmTeamHint.capability2: Each role uses preset tools and methods while the run card shows layers, tools, and progress
swarmTeamHint.capability3: Passes upstream findings into downstream review or synthesis instead of relying on one viewpoint
swarmTeamHint.footer: Best for complex work needing cross-checks; it usually takes longer and uses more model calls than normal chat.

agent.executablesNotAllowed: Executables and archives are not allowed
agent.failedToSend: Failed to send message, please retry.
agent.failedToContinue: Failed to continue goal, please retry.

runDetail.title: Run Detail
runDetail.runNotFoundDesc: The run directory may have been removed, or your browser may not have API access configured. Check that the API authentication key is set in Settings if accessing remotely.
runDetail.noChartDataDesc: The backtest engine may not have generated price data. Check the artifacts/ directory.
runDetail.pickSymbolToLoad: Pick a symbol to load chart data.

compare.title: Strategy Comparison
compare.selectTwoRuns: Select two runs to compare their metrics.

alphaZoo.title: Alpha Zoo
alphaZoo.browseDesc: Browse formula-driven cross-sectional signals from Qlib, the Kakushadze 101 set, GTJA 191, and the academic anomaly literature. Click any alpha to read its formula and source code, or run a bench to score the whole zoo on a universe and period.
alphaZoo.noAlphasMatch: No alphas match the current filters.
alphaZoo.scoreZoo: Score a zoo on a universe
alphaZoo.scoreDesc: Computes IC / IR for every alpha in the selected zoo over the chosen universe and period, then bucketizes them as alive / reversed / dead.
alphaZoo.compareDesc: Benches just the alphas you pick on a universe and period, then ranks them by IC / IR with the gap to the leader — far faster than benching a whole zoo when you only care about a shortlist.
alphaZoo.alphaIdsHint: Separate ids with commas or spaces. Tip: tick alphas in the catalogue and hit "Compare" to prefill this.
alphaZoo.enterAtLeast2: Enter at least 2 distinct alpha ids to compare.
alphaZoo.icPositiveTitle: Share of periods with positive IC

validation.monteCarloDesc: Shuffled trade order {{n}} times to test if Sharpe is better than random.
validation.bootstrapDesc: Resampled daily returns {{n}} times to estimate {{pct}} confidence interval.
validation.walkForwardDesc: Split into {{n}} sequential windows to check performance consistency.

runnerStatus.connectProfile: Connect this profile to enable connector runtime
runnerStatus.authorizeNote: The connector channel stays read-only until OAuth succeeds and a mandate is committed.
runnerStatus.noActiveMandate: No active mandate. Ask the agent to propose one, then commit it before starting the connector runtime.

mandate.noBroker: Cannot commit mandate: connector broker is missing. Ask the agent to regenerate the proposal.

messageBubble.pdfRendererUnavailable: The PDF renderer is unavailable. The system print dialog has been opened instead.
messageBubble.timeoutHint: Execution timed out. Try simplifying the strategy or reducing the number of assets.
messageBubble.apiFailedHint: API call failed. Please retry later.

pineViewer.footer: TradingView Pine Editor → New blank indicator → Paste code → Add to Chart

home.title: AI-Powered Quant Strategy Research
home.subtitle: Describe a trading strategy in natural language. The agent generates code, runs backtests, and optimizes — all in real time.
home.featureAgentDesc: Natural language strategy generation with ReAct reasoning
home.featureBacktestDesc: 7 data sources across A-shares, US/HK & Crypto
home.featureStreamingDesc: Watch the agent think, call tools, and iterate
home.featureReplayDesc: Trade journal analyzer + Shadow Account — extract your rules, backtest them, attribute PnL delta
